name: Route of the La Playa, del Pueyo, and del Pito Salt Lakes
name_original: Saladas de Sástago y Bujarlaoz — circular por los humedales de Aragón
description: Explore the unique Saladas de Sastago y Bujaraloz, a 6.8-mile moderate hike through Aragon's extensive saline wetlands.
region_intro: Hiking around Sástago reveals a landscape shaped by the Río Ebro and unique salt lagoons. The region features generally flat to gently rolling terrain, with low elevation gains making it accessible for various hiking abilities. Historical structures like towers and monasteries are also part of the natural and cultural scenery. Best hiking trails around Sástago The most popular hiking route is Salamanca Tower and Compromise Castle Trail, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 39 minutes to complete. This route combines natural paths with historical landmarks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laguna salada de Chiprana — circular, an easy 2.8 miles (4.5 km) path. This trail offers views of the unique salt lagoon landscape. Local hikers also love the Camí de Sant Jaume and GR 99 Loop from Rueda Monastery, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and past historical sites, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Hiking around Sástago is defined by the Río Ebro, salt lakes, and historical structures, offering diverse scenery. The network provides options for different ability levels, primarily featuring easy to moderate trails. The routes in Sástago are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 18 hikers have used komoot to explore Sástago's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Sástago?
A: Sástago offers a selection of 10 hiking routes. The majority, 6 of them, are classified as easy, while 4 are moderate, making the area accessible for various skill levels.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Sástago?
A: Yes, Sástago is well-suited for family hikes due to its generally flat to gently rolling terrain and low elevation gains. Many of the routes are easy, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167253173/laguna-salada-de-chiprana-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna salada de Chiprana — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.8 miles (4.5 km) long and offers views of the unique salt lagoon landscape.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Sástago?
A: Many trails in Sástago are suitable for dogs, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially near wildlife or agricultural areas, and to clean up after them. The region's open landscapes and riverine paths are generally pleasant for walks with pets.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Sástago?
A: Yes, Sástago features several circular routes. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167329538/from-alborge-to-cinco-olivas-circular-by-the-rio-ebro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Alborge to Cinco Olivas — circular by the Río Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.5-mile (8.8 km) trail that follows the Ebro river, offering scenic views and a complete loop back to your starting point.

Q: What kind of unique viewpoints can I expect on the trails around Sástago?
A: Sástago's trails offer unique perspectives on its natural and historical landscape. You can find viewpoints overlooking the distinctive salt lagoons, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336463" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Saladas de Sástago&lt;/a&gt;, or scenic spots along the Río Ebro. Historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5818549" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salamanca Tower&lt;/a&gt; also provide interesting sights.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking in Sástago?
A: The Sástago region is rich in history. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5818549" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salamanca Tower&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167210038/salamanca-tower-and-compromise-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salamanca Tower and Compromise Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5025865" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Torica Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Tambor of Sástago&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the area's past.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by the salt lakes?
A: Yes, the unique salt lagoons are a defining feature of the Sástago landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976152721/route-of-the-la-playa-del-pueyo-and-del-pito-salt-lakes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the La Playa, del Pueyo, and del Pito Salt Lakes&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.8-mile (11 km) trail specifically designed to showcase these natural wonders.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Sástago?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Sástago, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, which combines the tranquil Río Ebro, unique salt lagoons, and historical structures, offering a varied and engaging outdoor experience.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Sástago?
A: While Sástago can be enjoyed year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in the open areas around the Ebro river and salt lagoons, so early mornings or late afternoons are advisable during those months.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Camino de Santiago or other long-distance paths?
A: Yes, you can experience sections of significant long-distance routes in Sástago. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167308240/cami-de-sant-jaume-and-gr-99-loop-from-rueda-monastery" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí de Sant Jaume and GR 99 Loop from Rueda Monastery&lt;/a&gt; is a 4.6-mile (7.4 km) trail that incorporates parts of both the Camí de Sant Jaume and the GR 99, offering a blend of pilgrimage and riverine landscapes.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Sástago?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting near towns or popular attractions, you can often find designated parking areas. For more remote trails, roadside parking might be an option, but it's always best to check specific route details or local signage before you go.
tips: Las Saladas de Sástago - Bujaraloz are a treasure of the province of Zaragoza, recognized but not as resonant as other similar places in Aragon.

Beautiful views of the Laguna de la Playa (the largest and most representative of the salt lakes that make up the complex) that we find in the Saladas de Sástago, a unique saline landscape in Europe that fills up completely only in times of abundant rain

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Saladas de Sástago y Bujaraloz circular trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as easy, making it accessible for most fitness levels. The paths are generally straightforward and easy to navigate.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses a largely flat and dry steppe landscape, characterized by unpaved roads. You'll experience vast plains, primarily dedicated to cereal cultivation, interspersed with numerous temporary, saline lagoons. The scenery includes semi-naked and chalky hills, reflecting an arid, Monegros desert environment.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the Ruins of Las Saladas de Sástago and a dedicated View of the Saladas de Sástago. The largest and most recommended lagoon to visit is known as Salada de la Playa.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The area is a vital habitat for steppe bird species, including the Great Bustard, Little Bustard, Lesser Kestrel, Stone-curlew, and Dupont's Lark. Raptors such as the Golden Eagle are also present. When water levels are favorable, the wetlands attract a diverse array of aquatic birds and waders, making it particularly notable for birdwatching.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Saladas de Sástago y Bujaraloz?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit depends on your interests. While the trail is accessible year-round, it's important to note that the lagoons are frequently dry, especially in late spring and summer. For optimal birdwatching and to see the wetlands with more water, consider visiting outside of these drier periods.

faqs.question: Is the Saladas de Sástago y Bujaraloz trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog regulations for this trail is not provided. However, as it passes through a natural area known for sensitive birdlife, it is generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Reserva Natural Dirigida de las Saladas de Bujaraloz y Sástago?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access the Reserva Natural Dirigida de las Saladas de Bujaraloz y Sástago for hiking purposes. However, always respect local regulations and signage within protected areas.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route, meaning you can typically start from various points. Specific trailhead parking information is not provided, but access points near Sástago or Bujaraloz would be the most logical starting locations. It's recommended to check local maps or komoot's route details for precise starting coordinates and parking suggestions.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's natural and steppe environment, it is unlikely that there are dedicated restrooms or water sources directly along the route. Hikers should plan to bring their own water and be prepared for a lack of facilities.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The towns of Sástago and Bujaraloz, located near the wetlands, are likely to offer basic services such as bars, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options. It's advisable to check local listings for these towns if you plan to eat or stay overnight.

faqs.question: What makes the Saladas de Sástago y Bujaraloz wetlands ecologically significant?
faqs.answer: This area is considered the most extensive and important system of endorheic, temporary, and saline lagoons in Western Europe. Its significant ecological value is recognized by its inclusion in the Ramsar Convention as one of Aragon's four internationally important wetlands, primarily due to its unique geological features and exceptional birdlife.
